releases.title: v3.1.2
releases.summary: This patch release tightens the post-split shell experience without reopening shared-core architecture work. The auth page now uses a simpler single-column layout, and the Help and Help Admin surfaces carry less narration and less panel-heavy chrome, so platform and homelab feel more consistent and less overdesigned.

releases.title: v3.1.1
releases.summary: This patch release closes the confidence gap between the shared-core lifecycle work from `3.1.0` and what users actually see in the browser. The Playwright regression layer now proves browser-visible fallback and boundary behavior for support sessions, library lifecycle fallout, and space lifecycle fallout, so the extracted shared-core repair paths are no longer validated only by backend/runtime smokes.

releases.title: v3.1.0
releases.summary: This minor release turns the proven edition boundary into a more explicit product split by tightening the shared core that both platform and homelab consume. Shared auth, scope, library, and lifecycle behavior now follow one more disciplined runtime contract, while the platform and homelab shells are kept distinct where their product responsibilities differ.

details.heading: Shared-core extraction and scope normalization
bullets: Tightened shared auth/bootstrap behavior so effective scope resolution is derived more consistently from active library and accessible membership state instead of stale persisted workspace hints.
Consolidated more support-session, active-scope, and active-library normalization into shared helpers instead of leaving route-local repair logic scattered across auth and lifecycle flows.

Reduced drift between request-level effective scope, persisted profile state, and session-auth payload shaping so `/api/auth/me`, scope payloads, and related shared routes align on the same derived state.

details.heading: Lifecycle repair hardening
bullets: Extracted more library-access-loss and space-access-invalidation behavior into common service helpers so archive, delete, ownership transfer, suspension, and membership-removal flows reuse one repair path.
Tightened cleanup of stale active-library, support-library, current support-session, and previous support-session pointers when users lose access to a library or space.
Added targeted backend/runtime coverage to prove the extracted helpers repair real lifecycle fallout on the live stack.

details.heading: Edition split and shell boundary tightening
bullets: Made the edition contract more explicit in shared bootstrap/auth behavior so platform and homelab consume one authoritative definition of their allowed surfaces.
Continued separating platform-only route groups and shell behaviors from homelab-safe shared surfaces instead of relying on raw role checks or hidden-but-mounted behavior.
Hardened the local maintainer workflow so platform and homelab run as first-class parallel compose targets during split verification rather than toggling one shared stack back and forth.

details.heading: Public homelab split preparation
bullets: Kept the private platform control plane intact while tightening the shared core needed for a later public homelab repo promotion path.
Focused this release on safe extraction, contract hardening, and edition verification rather than public repo publishing mechanics.

releases.title: v3.0.0
releases.summary: This major release completes the frontend build modernization from Create React App to Vite. The app now builds and runs through a single Vite-based toolchain for local development and production static assets, the nginx-served frontend image uses Vite output directly, the legacy CRA fallback rail is removed, and the repo has been re-proven against the live Docker stack and browser-regression suite on the final Vite-only state.

releases.title: v2.11.0
releases.summary: This minor release adds the first optional market valuation foundation for collectZ. Workspace-scoped integrations can now carry valuation provider configuration, media records can store normalized read-only value bands, the library detail drawer can display and manually refresh valuation data, and imports can queue non-blocking follow-on valuation jobs for newly created media. The milestone keeps pricing optional, read-only, and safely decoupled from core catalog CRUD and import flows.

releases.title: v2.10.17
releases.summary: This patch release finishes the workspace-owned member lifecycle foundation. Workspace owners and admins can now force password resets, suspend and restore members within the workspace boundary, invite a brand-new person to become the initial owner during workspace creation, and remove a member from a workspace without silently deleting the content they contributed there. The release keeps platform-global administration distinct from workspace-scoped lifecycle control and preserves the intended tenancy model.

releases.title: v2.10.16
releases.summary: This patch release turns the SaaS auth surface into a real public account-entry flow. Users can now request password resets from the sign-in screen, self-register when the platform allows it, verify their email before normal sign-in, and land in the correct workspace model depending on how they were onboarded. Public self-registration now creates a personal workspace after verification, while invite-based onboarding still lands a new user only in the invited workspace without creating an extra personal one.

releases.title: v2.10.15
releases.summary: This patch release establishes a real platform-owned SMTP delivery model for the SaaS product. Platform operators can now see whether email delivery is configured, manage a masked app-level SMTP override on top of environment defaults, and validate live delivery with a protected test-send flow before the next registration, password-reset, and workspace invite milestones build on top of it.

releases.title: v2.10.14
releases.summary: This patch release turns the product’s space-separation promise into real workspace behavior. Settings, integrations, and activity now belong to the current workspace instead of quietly depending on global admin surfaces, while the platform layer is narrowed toward true platform concerns.

releases.title: v2.10.13
releases.summary: This patch release finishes the remaining `2.10.x` shared-language cleanup by moving the app away from display-font chrome and auth-page mini-marketing toward calmer, more product-native shared UI. Workspace headings, panel headings, and the authentication surface now follow the same quieter language instead of drifting into uppercase dashboard theatrics.
